description: Mapping of CCPA/CPRA requirements to NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0

source.disclaimer: IMPORTANT: These mappings are interpretive guidance created to assist with compliance research. They are NOT official California Attorney General or NIST crosswalks. CCPA/CPRA is a privacy regulation while NIST CSF is a cybersecurity framework - overlap exists but is not complete. For authoritative guidance, consult: (1) California Privacy Protection Agency regulations and guidance, (2) NIST Privacy Framework for privacy-specific controls, (3) Your organization's compliance and legal counsel. These mappings should be validated against your specific implementation context.
references: California Consumer Privacy Act (Cal. Civ. Code §§ 1798.100-199)
source.methodology: Mappings identify NIST CSF functions and categories that support CCPA/CPRA compliance objectives. Privacy requirements mapped to closest cybersecurity controls. Coverage indicates degree of alignment: 'full' = strong alignment, 'partial' = supporting control.

mappings.rationale: Privacy program roles and responsibilities align with governance
Right to deletion requires data lifecycle management
Right to know requires data inventory and mapping
Disclosure requirements align with transparency goals
Sensitive personal information controls require protection
Non-discrimination provisions relate to privacy governance
Opt-out mechanisms require rights management processes
Privacy notice requirements align with policy establishment
Data protection measures required for consumer information
Service provider requirements align with supply chain management
Consumer verification processes require identity management
Enforcement procedures align with rights response
Data breach notification aligns with incident communication
